title: Dominium Universal Reality Framework

framework_principles.name: truth_perceived_render_boundary
framework_principles.summary: Truth is authoritative, Perceived is derived observer-scoped state, and Rendered is presentation-only state.
norms: Perceived state must not redefine or mutate Truth.
Rendered state must not mutate Truth or decide ontology, law, or authority.

framework_principles.name: truth_totality
framework_principles.summary: Truth is total and authoritative within declared world scope and lawful horizons.
norms: Unmaterialized truth is not undefined.
Undefined state is lawful only when explicitly declared out of scope or absent by framework or domain contract.

framework_principles.name: sparse_materialization
framework_principles.summary: Computation and detail realization may be deferred, but authoritative commitments required for the current horizon may not be deferred.
norms: Performance-oriented deferral must not alter semantic meaning.
Law, identity, provenance, conservation, and replay-critical commitments remain authoritative even when detail is latent.

framework_principles.name: minimal_causal_closure
framework_principles.summary: Resolved state must be causally sufficient for the relevant interaction and observation horizon.
norms: If a representation cannot answer the lawful question set without guesswork, it must be expanded, refused, or escalated.
Causal closure is the minimum resolved state sufficient to preserve lawful outcomes and observation at the active horizon.

framework_principles.name: epistemic_and_diegetic_refinement
framework_principles.summary: Observation and refinement may depend on authority, law, epistemic artifacts, and deterministic policies, but not on silent privilege or convenience.
norms: Camera zoom, renderer privilege, wall-clock time, and thread timing are not lawful refinement authorities.
Ignorance and secrecy are epistemic conditions, not edits to objective truth.

framework_principles.name: representation_integrity
framework_principles.summary: Multiple representations are lawful only when they preserve declared invariants and do not silently redefine meaning.
norms: Optimization may change representation but not semantics silently.
Every downstream representation must declare preserved invariants, summarized commitments, and required expansion conditions.

framework_principles.name: ladder_families
framework_principles.summary: Primitive, recognized, formalized, substituted, and macro/micro ladder families are lawful views over one substrate rather than separate realities.
norms: Ladder transitions must remain traceable and semantically aligned.
Later Λ prompts may refine ladder detail but may not reopen the ladder family floor.

framework_principles.name: semantic_ascent_and_descent
framework_principles.summary: Higher-order meaning must emerge from lawful substrate and be expandable or auditable back into operational detail.
norms: Ascent must be grounded in lawful recognition and provenance.
Descent must preserve committed meaning or explicit uncertainty; it must not invent detail.

framework_principles.name: lawful_substitution_and_capsules
framework_principles.summary: Substitution and capsules are lawful condensed forms only when invariants, continuity, and causal closure are preserved.
norms: Capsules are canonical condensed truth forms, not fake worlds.
Substitution is invalid if it changes meaning silently, loses identity/provenance anchors, or breaks conservation/topology commitments.

framework_principles.name: experiential_continuity
framework_principles.summary: Expansion, collapse, substitution, migration, and projection changes must preserve lawful player-observable continuity.
norms: Optimization must not create arbitrary teleportation, hidden retcons, or unexplained contradictions.
Continuity is semantic and causal, not pixel-identity.

framework_principles.name: macro_micro_consistency
framework_principles.summary: Macro and micro are different lawful granularities over one truth substrate and must preserve committed invariants across expansion and collapse.
norms: Macro must summarize enough to keep future expansion lawful.
Micro expansion must preserve conserved quantities, topology, identity, provenance, and already-committed lawful constraints.

framework_principles.name: interface_agnostic_reality
framework_principles.summary: Reality is not tied to one interface mode and must support embodied players, planners, AI agents, NPC labor, and future interfaces.
norms: Interfaces may change access path, observation, and presentation, but not core world semantics.
Capability surfaces later formalize interface access without allowing interface-specific ontology.

framework_principles.name: domain_inheritance
framework_principles.summary: All later domain constitutions inherit this framework and may specialize it without silently overriding core reality semantics.
norms: Domains may define specific conserved quantities, refinement triggers, and refusal conditions.
Domains may not locally redefine Truth, sparse materialization, causal closure, substitution validity, continuity, or interface-agnostic reality.

continuity_requirements: identity anchors remain stable across expansion, collapse, substitution, migration, and projection changes
observable cause and effect remain consistent with world law
changes in fidelity or access must be explainable by lawful refinement or observation conditions
macro and micro transitions preserve committed invariants and enough summary state for lawful future expansion
optimization may change representation but must not create unexplained world jumps or retcons

sigma: governance and task systems must preserve truth/perceived/render separation
agent and wrapper systems may not redefine ontology
refusal, redaction, and capability gating are lawful semantic outcomes
phi: optimization is lawful only through representation and substitution that preserve invariants
runtime services must treat latent truth as authoritative substrate rather than undefined empty space
snapshot, replay, and state externalization work must preserve identity, provenance, and continuity
upsilon_and_zeta: release, migration, rollback, and live operations may not silently alter semantic meaning
deployment and coexistence logic must preserve continuity and explicit identity
cutover or downgrade flows must not violate the invariant-preserving substitution rule

later_lambda_refinements.role: define reusable domain contract template under the inheritance rule
formalize capability surfaces over the universal framework
refine representation ladders and semantic ascent/descent detail
define formalization chain and substitution proof criteria
define player desire acceptance and refusal mapping
define cross-domain bridges after semantic ownership review
